title: sync_guard.rs: check_lag rejects scan_tip=0 but passes scan_tip=1 with chain_tip=0 (reorg to genesis)
description: sync_guard.rs check_lag guards: first checks local_tip == 0 (never synced), then checks chain_tip.saturating_sub(local_tip) > max_lag_blocks. The comment says reorg (chain_tip < local_tip) is handled by saturating_sub returning 0. But the check also passes when local_tip=1 and chain_tip=0 (deep reorg to genesis), which is a valid reorg but means the wallet thinks it has one block of knowledge while the chain has none. The scan_tip=1, chain_tip=0 case produces lag=0 and proceeds to send_payment. Spendable notes at height 1 are actually not yet confirmed (chain tip is 0). This is an extremely unlikely edge case (chain rolled back to genesis) but could theoretically allow spending notes that are no longer in the canonical chain. Not blocking. Document the accepted limitation in check_lag.
